description:
During Atlas adoption of feature/realized-by-triangle-closure, a proof conversion of search.keyword exposed a possible mismatch between SpecLedEx tagged_tests semantics and ExUnit filtering.

Observed behavior:
- A test tagged with @tag spec: ["search.keyword.tsvector_search", "search.keyword.identifier_splitting"] was not selected by a repeated ExUnit filter command like:
  mix test --only spec:search.keyword.tsvector_search --only spec:search.keyword.identifier_splitting --only spec:search.keyword.batch_search --only spec:search.keyword.bulk_index
- That run selected only 5 of the 6 intended proof tests.
- Changing the test to a single literal tag, @tag spec: "search.keyword.tsvector_search", and relying on separate identifier-splitting tests made the same command select all 6 intended tests.

Investigation questions:
- Should SpecLedEx support one test proving multiple requirement IDs through a list-valued @tag spec?
- If yes, tagged_tests command generation may need to account for ExUnit list-valued tag matching semantics instead of assuming --only spec:<id> will match list membership.
- If no, docs and validation should probably say that @tag spec: values must be single literal requirement IDs, or emit a warning/error for list-valued spec tags.

Expected outcome:
- Decide and document the supported tag shape.
- Add a regression fixture/test for tagged_tests selection, either proving list-valued spec tags work or proving they produce an actionable validation finding.

Context: Atlas commit ac2bbbe on atlas-r59.1/specled-tagged-tests used single literal tags as the safe migration pattern.
title: Investigate list-valued spec tags in tagged_tests verification
